title: DSP-7709: add account-level Databricks budgets and budget policies
description:
## Summary

- **`databricks_budget`** — one account-level budget per workspace in `workspaces`, with monthly threshold **`budget_monthly_threshold_usd`** (Luna default `1000` USD). Alerts use framework tag filters + **`budget_alert_email`** / **`budget_time_period`** (Terraform defaults unless overridden via tfvars).
- **`databricks_budget_policy`** — **one** account-level serverless policy, **`{label}-serverless-budget-policy`**, with **`binding_workspace_ids`** = all managed workspaces and framework **`custom_tags`**. This replaces per-workspace policy resources so we avoid parallel account API creates that returned misleading **Duplicate policy name found** errors ([terraform-provider-databricks#4600](https://github.com/databricks/terraform-provider-databricks/issues/4600)).
- Budget controls are **always on** for configured workspaces (no separate feature flag variable).
- **Provision (minimal):** deployer **`roles/billing.admin`** alongside CloudZero on the default account rule set (`cloudzero.tf`); Databricks provider **`>=1.112.0, <2.0`** in provision and aligned layers/modules.
- **Metastore / provider alignment:** metastore zip bumps where consumed; JDBC SP module uses `configuration_aliases` for `databricks.account`.

## Outputs

- `budget_configuration_ids` — per-workspace budget configuration ids  
- `budget_policy_id` — single shared serverless budget policy id (or null if no workspaces)

## Diff vs `origin/qa` (high level)

**32 files**, ~208 insertions / ~43 deletions.

| Area | Files |
|------|--------|
| Budgets + policy | `configurations/budgeting.tf`, `variables.tf`, `outputs.tf`, `deploy/luna-config-spec.yaml` |
| Provision (scoped) | `provision/cloudzero.tf`, `provision/versions.tf`, `provision/unity.tf` |
| Other Databricks layers/modules | `connector_deployer`, `mdm`, `object_permission`, `tokens`, workflows, `unity_catalog`, `workspace_configurations`, `jdbc_sp_configurations`, etc. |
| Metastore consumers | `storage/common/s3.tf`, `security/roles|policies/metastore.tf`, `job_metadata_db` |
| Docs | `README.md` |

## Test plan

- [x] Deploy `nextgen.databricks.provision` then `nextgen.databricks.configurations` via Seiji; apply succeeds.
- [x] Account console: budgets and **one** serverless budget policy as expected.
- [ ] Confirm FinOps / alert email receives budget notifications if thresholds are exceeded (as applicable).

## Rollout / cleanup

If earlier failed applies left **per-workspace** serverless policy names (`{label}-{workspace}-serverless-budget-policy`) in the account without state, remove them in the Account console (or import) so they do not confuse operators; the managed policy name is **`{label}-serverless-budget-policy`** only.

diffs.diff:
@@ -0,0 +1,10 @@
+#!/bin/bash
+set -euo pipefail
+
+# Compatibility migration.
+# Seiji migration DB for some tenants already references this filename.
+# Keep this script present and idempotent so migration initialization does not fail
+# when older deploy attempts registered 013 in the DB.
+
+echo "[INFO] 013-remove-budget-policy: no-op compatibility migration"
+echo "[INFO] Nothing to migrate; preserving script for migration DB continuity."

@@ -123,6 +123,24 @@ config:
       required: true
       tfvar: true
       type: str
+    databricks_partner_end_customer:
+      default: "Abacus Insights"
+      description: "The customer on whose behalf we are bricking that sweet, sweet data."
+      required: false
+      tfvar: true
+      type: str
+    databricks_partner_end_customer_id:
+      default: "internal"
+      description: "The ID corresponding to databricks_partner_end_customer."
+      required: false
+      tfvar: true
+      type: str
+    budget_monthly_threshold_usd:
+      default: 1000
+      description: "Monthly Databricks account budget threshold in USD (list price) for each workspace budget."
+      required: false
+      tfvar: true
+      type: int
     workspace_to_gitlab_branch:
       default:
         dev: develop

@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
 provider "databricks" {
-  alias = "workspace-managing-data-access"
+  alias   = "workspace-managing-data-access"
   profile = "${local.label}-${var.workspace_managing_data_access}"
 }
 
@@ -16,8 +16,8 @@ resource "databricks_grant" "system_catalog_queries" {
   for_each = toset(local.principals_with_system_access)
   provider = databricks.workspace-managing-data-access
 
-  catalog   = "system"
-  principal = each.value
+  catalog    = "system"
+  principal  = each.value
   privileges = ["USE_CATALOG", "USE_SCHEMA", "SELECT"]
 }
 
@@ -25,8 +25,8 @@ resource "databricks_grant" "system_catalog_uses" {
   for_each = toset(local.principals_with_no_system_access)
   provider = "databricks.workspace-managing-data-access"
 
-  catalog   = "system"
-  principal = each.value
+  catalog    = "system"
+  principal  = each.value
   privileges = ["USE_CATALOG"]
 }
 
@@ -36,6 +36,6 @@ resource "databricks_grant" "system_catalog_uses" {
 resource "databricks_system_schema" "system_schema" {
   for_each = var.system_schemas_enabled
   provider = "databricks.workspace-managing-data-access"
-  schema = each.value
+  schema   = each.value
 
 }

@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 module "metastore" {
   count                 = var.metastore_id == "" ? 1 : 0
-  source                = "http://localhost:6422/object/releases/databricks-metastore/databricks-metastore-26.2.0.zip//metastore"
+  source                = "http://localhost:6422/object/releases/databricks-metastore/databricks-metastore-26.3.0.zip//metastore"
   customer              = local.customer
   metastore_bucket_name = data.terraform_remote_state.storage_common.outputs.metastore_bucket_name
   region                = var.region

@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 module "metastore" {
   count                = var.metastore_id == "" ? 1 : 0
-  source               = "http://localhost:6422/object/releases/databricks-metastore/databricks-metastore-25.4.0.zip//policies"
+  source               = "http://localhost:6422/object/releases/databricks-metastore/databricks-metastore-26.3.0.zip//policies"
   metastore_bucket_arn = data.terraform_remote_state.storage_common.outputs.metastore_bucket_arn
   metastore_key_arn    = data.terraform_remote_state.storage_common.outputs.metastore_key_arn
   metastore_role_arn   = data.terraform_remote_state.security_roles.outputs.metastore_role_arn

@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 module "metastore" {
   count                 = var.metastore_id == "" ? 1 : 0
-  source                = "http://localhost:6422/object/releases/databricks-metastore/databricks-metastore-25.4.0.zip//role"
+  source                = "http://localhost:6422/object/releases/databricks-metastore/databricks-metastore-26.3.0.zip//role"
   databricks_account_id = var.databricks_account_id
   label                 = local.label
   layer_tag_map         = local.layer_tag_map

@@ -386,7 +386,7 @@ resource "aws_s3_object" "adhoc" {
 
 module "metastore" {
   count                               = var.metastore_id == "" ? 1 : 0
-  source                              = "http://localhost:6422/object/releases/databricks-metastore/databricks-metastore-25.7.0.zip//bucket"
+  source                              = "http://localhost:6422/object/releases/databricks-metastore/databricks-metastore-26.3.0.zip//bucket"
   allowed_cidrs                       = local.admin_plus_databricks_cidrs
   allowed_databricks_s3_endpoint_vpcs = local.databricks_control_plane_s3_endpoint_vpcs[data.aws_region.current.name]

@@ -1,5 +1 @@
-# these providers should be configured with a `providers` block on the module call
-
-provider "databricks" {
-  alias = "account"
-}
\ No newline at end of file
+# these providers should be configured with a `providers` block on the module call
\ No newline at end of file

@@ -120,6 +120,23 @@ example, to change the URL to the repository containing the connector files, the
         connector_repository_url: "https://gitlab.com/abacusinsights/abacus-v2/next-gen-pipelines/<some-arbitrary-location>"
    ```
 
+## Databricks budgets and serverless budget policy
+
+Defined in `terraform/layers/databricks/configurations/budgeting.tf`:
+
+- **`databricks_budget`** — one monthly budget per workspace in `workspaces`, threshold from **`budget_monthly_threshold_usd`** (Luna default `1000` USD). Alerts use **`budget_alert_email`** / **`budget_time_period`** (Terraform defaults; override via tfvars if needed).
+- **`databricks_budget_policy`** — single account policy **`{label}-serverless-budget-policy`** binding all workspace IDs (avoids parallel per-workspace policy API flakes; see [terraform-provider-databricks#4600](https://github.com/databricks/terraform-provider-databricks/issues/4600)).
+
+Framework tags on budgets and policy:
+
+- `databricks_partner=Abacus Insights`
+- `databricks_partner_end_customer`
+- `databricks_partner_end_customer_id`
+
+Outputs: **`budget_configuration_ids`**, **`budget_policy_id`**.
+
+**Orphans:** old per-workspace policy names from prior attempts may need console cleanup or import if names conflict. If account API flakes return, try a one-off `terraform apply -parallelism=1` locally or reintroduce that flag in `create.sh`.
+
 ## Naming Conventions
 
 The following conventions should be obeyed when creating new named resources anywhere in

[2026-03-26-20-51-51.log](/uploads/3a97b0b83cf3a01aa9c1dc82c4660193/2026-03-26-20-51-51.log) is the log of my successful deploy with the buget policies in the output, but cannot seem to get access to budget policy page in the account console UI.

changed the description

Few questions:

1. Is this supposed be a feature flag controlled option or is the intended behaviour supposed to just be enabled everywhere?
2. Since this is also defined in `luna-config-spec` and has the default value already, we don't need to have the `default = true` here

I think this should be on by default, but we can use a feature flag/config value to manage the “per account” budget. Then FinOps can set that value themselves per tenant.

And heard re: not repeating the value from luna-config-spec

Yeah, my next follow up was going to ask about this:
 `budget_managed_workspaces = var.budget_controls_enabled ? var.workspaces : toset([])`

The way it is setup is suited for feature flags otherwise with a default true it applies to every workspace

changed this line in [version 25 of the diff](/abacusinsights/abacus-v2/next-gen-platform/ng-infrastructure/-/merge_requests/1618/diffs?diff_id=1728274603&start_sha=ad3f147360ecec73e87c0e0f7140300baa25b549#e55c062406b562b2aba31d53c6cc83c984fe438a_96_92)

@inderjit.bassi I have updated the luna config + variables to only allow us to manage the budget per account, and turned on these budget policies by default in all our tenants.

AFAICT, this is only adding more monitoring around our Databricks usage, which IMO is a good to have wherever we're running databricks: https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/admin/usage/budget-policies

approvals.title: DSP-7709: add account-level Databricks budgets and budget policies
approvals.description:
## Summary

- **`databricks_budget`** — one account-level budget per workspace in `workspaces`, with monthly threshold **`budget_monthly_threshold_usd`** (Luna default `1000` USD). Alerts use framework tag filters + **`budget_alert_email`** / **`budget_time_period`** (Terraform defaults unless overridden via tfvars).
- **`databricks_budget_policy`** — **one** account-level serverless policy, **`{label}-serverless-budget-policy`**, with **`binding_workspace_ids`** = all managed workspaces and framework **`custom_tags`**. This replaces per-workspace policy resources so we avoid parallel account API creates that returned misleading **Duplicate policy name found** errors ([terraform-provider-databricks#4600](https://github.com/databricks/terraform-provider-databricks/issues/4600)).
- Budget controls are **always on** for configured workspaces (no separate feature flag variable).
- **Provision (minimal):** deployer **`roles/billing.admin`** alongside CloudZero on the default account rule set (`cloudzero.tf`); Databricks provider **`>=1.112.0, <2.0`** in provision and aligned layers/modules.
- **Metastore / provider alignment:** metastore zip bumps where consumed; JDBC SP module uses `configuration_aliases` for `databricks.account`.

## Outputs

- `budget_configuration_ids` — per-workspace budget configuration ids  
- `budget_policy_id` — single shared serverless budget policy id (or null if no workspaces)

## Diff vs `origin/qa` (high level)

**32 files**, ~208 insertions / ~43 deletions.

| Area | Files |
|------|--------|
| Budgets + policy | `configurations/budgeting.tf`, `variables.tf`, `outputs.tf`, `deploy/luna-config-spec.yaml` |
| Provision (scoped) | `provision/cloudzero.tf`, `provision/versions.tf`, `provision/unity.tf` |
| Other Databricks layers/modules | `connector_deployer`, `mdm`, `object_permission`, `tokens`, workflows, `unity_catalog`, `workspace_configurations`, `jdbc_sp_configurations`, etc. |
| Metastore consumers | `storage/common/s3.tf`, `security/roles|policies/metastore.tf`, `job_metadata_db` |
| Docs | `README.md` |

## Test plan

- [x] Deploy `nextgen.databricks.provision` then `nextgen.databricks.configurations` via Seiji; apply succeeds.
- [x] Account console: budgets and **one** serverless budget policy as expected.
- [ ] Confirm FinOps / alert email receives budget notifications if thresholds are exceeded (as applicable).

## Rollout / cleanup

If earlier failed applies left **per-workspace** serverless policy names (`{label}-{workspace}-serverless-budget-policy`) in the account without state, remove them in the Account console (or import) so they do not confuse operators; the managed policy name is **`{label}-serverless-budget-policy`** only.
